vulnerabilities.description: SHA-1 signature algorithm support for backward compatibility. SHA-1 is cryptographically broken due to collision vulnerabilities.
vulnerabilities.fix: Remove SHA-1 support entirely or require explicit opt-in with security warning. Default AllowSHA1Signatures to false.

vulnerabilities.description: 7-day refresh token expiration is excessively long, increasing window of abuse if tokens are stolen.
vulnerabilities.fix: Reduce refresh token duration to 24-48 hours maximum. Implement refresh token rotation.

vulnerabilities.description: No visible rate limiting on OAuth/OIDC endpoints, enabling brute force attacks.
vulnerabilities.fix: Implement rate limiting middleware on all authentication endpoints.

vulnerabilities.description: No token revocation mechanism visible for compromised tokens.
vulnerabilities.fix: Implement token blacklist/jti claim with Redis backend for revocation.

vulnerabilities.description: SameSite cookie attribute not enforced to Strict mode for CSRF protection.
vulnerabilities.fix: Default to SameSiteStrict mode in production for maximum CSRF protection.

vulnerabilities.description: Password hashing parameters are OWASP compliant but no pepper implementation.
vulnerabilities.fix: Consider adding a secret pepper value stored separately from hashes for additional defense in depth.

vulnerabilities.description: XML parsing without explicit protection against XXE (XML External Entity) attacks.
vulnerabilities.fix: Ensure xml.Decoder is configured with Entity resolution disabled.

owasp_checks.detail: JWT validation present but no token revocation mechanism found.
SHA-1 present in SAML for backward compatibility.
Parameterized queries expected, ORM usage recommended.
Long refresh token window (7 days) increases risk.
Strong password hashing (Argon2id) but no rate limiting visible.

owasp_checks.category: A08:2021 – Software and Data Integrity Failures
owasp_checks.detail: HMAC state validation in OAuth flow.
OpenTelemetry telemetry present but auth-specific logging not verified.
No user-controlled URLs in outbound requests visible.

critical_fixes: Remove SHA-1 signature support from SAML or default AllowSHA1Signatures to false
Reduce refresh token expiration from 7 days to maximum 48 hours
Add rate limiting to all authentication endpoints
